trigger_keywords: what if the URI is a file://?
how do you handle file URIs?
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The output will be the local absolute path (e.g. /home/develop/source.txt) after stripping the 'file://' prefix and resolving any relative segments if necessary, or simply the path component depending on the specific implementation details of 'file://' in this context.

context_pointers.vague_reference: follow the same serialization style as used in the previous YAML round-trip tests
context_pointers.actual_meaning: Output the resulting JSON object using 표준 Dart JSON encoding with 2-space indentation and no trailing commas.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_string_conversions.json.description: Convert identifier-like text into human-readable, capitalized, and hyphen-separated forms.

cases.expected_output:
input=camelCaseString
camel_text=camel case string
capitalized=CamelCaseString
kebab=camelCaseString
---
input=CamelCaseString
camel_text=camel case string
capitalized=CamelCaseString
kebab=CamelCaseString
---
input=snake_case
camel_text=snake_case
capitalized=Snake_case
kebab=snake-case
---
input=snake_case_string
camel_text=snake_case_string
capitalized=Snake_case_string
kebab=snake-case-string
---
input=kebab-case
camel_text=kebab-case
capitalized=Kebab-case
kebab=kebab-case
---

feature2_1_path_uri_conversion.json.description: Convert absent, file, and package-style URIs into nullable normalized filesystem paths.

cases.expected_output:
path=null
path=/home/develop/source.txt
path=/testbed/quality_tool/src/utils/path_utils.dart

feature2_2_exclude_patterns.json.description: Prepare relative exclude patterns under a project root and test whether a normalized path matches the configured excludes.

cases.expected_output:
pattern=/home/user/project/.dart_tool/**
pattern=/home/user/project/packages/**
pattern=/home/user/project/src/exclude_me.dart
excluded=true

feature3_yaml_config_conversion.json.description: Convert nested YAML configuration into a JSON object while preserving strings, numbers, lists, and maps.

input.content:
code_checker:
  metrics:
    cyclomatic-complexity: 20
    number-of-methods: 8
  metrics-exclude:
    - test/**
  rules:
    - no-boolean-literal-compare

cases.expected_output: {"code_checker":{"metrics":{"cyclomatic-complexity":20,"number-of-methods":8},"metrics-exclude":["test/**"],"rules":["no-boolean-literal-compare"]}}

feature4_1_severity_mapping.json.description: Map case-insensitive severity text to canonical severity labels, using none for blank or unknown text and null for an absent value.

severity=style
severity=warning
severity=error
severity=none
severity=null

feature6_2_magic_numbers_rule.json.description: Analyze source text and report unapproved numeric literals used directly in expressions, while ignoring constants and configured allowed values.

input.code:

bool bad_f2(int x) => x != 12; // LINT
void bad_f4(int x) => a * 3.14; // LINT
void bad_f5() => bad_f4(12); // LINT

issue_count=4

feature6_8_throw_inside_catch_rule.json.description: Analyze exception-handling code and report throw statements inside catch handlers because they discard the original failure context.

cases.expected_output:
rule=avoid-throw-in-catch-block
severity=warning
issue_count=2
issue[0].line=17
issue[0].column=5
issue[0].text=throw RepositoryException()
issue[0].message=Call throw in a catch block loses the original stack trace and the original exception.
issue[1].line=25
issue[1].column=5
issue[1].text=throw DataProviderException()
issue[1].message=Call throw in a catch block loses the original stack trace and the original exception.

_constraints.output_rule: Execute the corresponding feature logic; invalid commands result in no output or error (defensive)

_constraints.input_condition: input.command == 'convert_strings' AND input.values contains a string
_constraints.output_rule: Generate camel_text by splitting camelCase into lower-case words and joining with space

_constraints.input_condition: input.command == 'convert_strings' AND input.values contains a string
_constraints.output_rule: Generate capitalized by taking the original string and capitalizing the first character

_constraints.input_condition: input.command == 'convert_strings' AND input.values contains a string
_constraints.output_rule: Generate kebab by converting underscores to hyphens (snake_case -> snake-case)
Output lines: input=<v>, camel_text=<v>, capitalized=<v>, kebab=<v>, then separator '---'
Output path=null

_constraints.feature: URI to Path - File URI Handling
_constraints.input_condition: input.command == 'normalize_paths' AND input.uris contains file://... URI
_constraints.output_rule: Output path=<filesystem_path_without_scheme>

_constraints.feature: URI to Path - Package/General URI Handling
_constraints.input_condition: input.command == 'normalize_paths' AND input.uris contains package:/ URI or other file:// URI
_constraints.output_rule: Resolve against current working directory (assumed testbed/root) or extract path component
Backslashes (\) must be converted to forward slashes (/) in stdout
Output pattern=<root>/<pattern> for each pattern in input.patterns
Output excluded=true/false based on whether path matches any pattern in input.patterns
Output valid JSON object representing the YAML structure
Object keys must be rendered in stable sorted order

_constraints.input_condition: input.command == 'severity_from_text' AND input.values contains non-empty non-null string
_constraints.output_rule: Map to lower-case canonical: none, style, warning, error

_constraints.input_condition: input.command == 'severity_from_text' AND input.values contains empty string or null
_constraints.output_rule: Empty string -> none; Null -> null
Output global:rule_name=<true/false>
Output line:<line>:rule_name=<true/false>
Output name=<input_name>, reporter=<canonical_name>

_constraints.input_condition: input.command == 'select_...' AND input.names contains empty string or unknown alias
_constraints.output_rule: Output name=, reporter=none
Output rule=..., severity=..., issue_count=<N>

_constraints.feature: Analyzer Output - Empty Block - Empty Body
_constraints.input_condition: input.command == 'analyze_rule' AND rule='empty-blocks' AND block has no statements/comments
_constraints.output_rule: Report issue with line/column, text={}, and specific message
exact_values: Block is empty. Empty blocks are often indicators of missing code.

_constraints.feature: Analyzer Output - Empty Block - Whitespace Only
_constraints.input_condition: input.command == 'analyze_rule' AND rule='empty-blocks' AND block has whitespace only
_constraints.output_rule: Report issue preserving whitespace in text field

_constraints.feature: Analyzer Output - Empty Block - Ignore Comments
_constraints.input_condition: input.command == 'analyze_rule' AND rule='empty-blocks' AND block contains at least one comment
_constraints.output_rule: Do NOT output issues for this block
Detect numeric literals in executable expressions (constants, collections, etc. excluded)

_constraints.input_condition: input.command == 'analyze_rule' AND rule='magic-numbers' AND input.config.allowed present
_constraints.output_rule: Ignore numeric literals present in input.config.allowed

_constraints.input_condition: All features except analyzer (which uses custom delimiters)
_constraints.output_rule: Field remained: line breaks, key=value pairs
Case-insensitive matching for inputs (e.g., nOne matches none)
Glob patterns (e.g., **/) are prepended with root and matched against paths

hide.replacement: a specific filesystem path derived from removing the file:// scheme
trigger_keywords: what if the URI is a file://?
how do you handle file URIs?
hide.oracle_response: The output will be the local absolute path (e.g. /home/develop/source.txt) after stripping the 'file://' prefix and resolving any relative segments if necessary, or simply the path component depending on the specific implementation details of 'file://' in this context.

vague_refs.vague_phrase: follow the same serialization style as used in the previous YAML round-trip tests
vague_refs.actual_meaning: Output the resulting JSON object using 표준 Dart JSON encoding with 2-space indentation and no trailing commas.
